changes.reason: Batch B isolated take-profit tuning. The prior squeeze capture move improved objective and net profit, but current squeeze_long has only 5 trades with excellent efficiency and median selected TP around 38.75, so the 7.5 cap may still be constraining upside. Move one grid step higher without changing entry filters.
Keep the current base TP at the tested 4.25 grid midpoint so this run isolates dynamic cap expansion rather than mixing target distance and entry selectivity again.
Keep slope confirmation at 0.05 from the prior accepted squeeze experiment to avoid loosening entries while testing whether more dynamic target room improves payoff capture.

expected_effect: Should improve squeeze_long average PnL and total net profit if the squeeze profile is exiting too early on confirmed continuation. Trade count should remain stable because entry confirmation is unchanged. Reject if exposure rises materially, squeeze hold time lengthens without PnL gain, worst MAE worsens beyond the current leader baseline, or total net profit falls below 98% of run_001.
label: squeeze-dynamic-cap-probe
rationale: Choose Batch B as a single-family continuation because the latest accepted squeeze experiment was one of the few recent changes that improved both score and net profit without trade starvation. Squeeze_long is low sample but high quality: 5 VALID_0DTE trades, $477.7 PnL, 424.9 PnL/exposure hour, and only -113.9 worst MAE. This is a controlled cap-only upside probe, not a broad filter or pullback re-expansion.

strategy_hypotheses.evidence: pullback_long remains the largest PnL engine at $2299.7 but owns the current -638.75 worst MAE. Broad structure pruning would block profitable pullbacks, and run_018 showed pullback TP/VWAP re-expansion can create severe drawdown and slow exposure.
strategy_hypotheses.name: Pullback Deep-Heat Companion Classifier
strategy_hypotheses.risk: Config-only proxies may block profitable weak-structure pullbacks; early-path and profile-aware state features are likely needed to avoid suppressing the core edge.
strategy_hypotheses.trigger: VALID_0DTE pullback_long entries with weak structure distances, low or negative VWAP acceleration, no early MFE, and delayed recovery behavior.
strategy_hypotheses.validation_metric: Retain at least 90% of pullback_long PnL while improving pullback worst MAE from -638.75 toward <= -527 and keeping total PnL/exposure flat or better.

strategy_hypotheses.evidence: All current bad-MAE rows are covered by the extended-VWAP near-miss family, but protected extended-VWAP winners contribute $720.8 with median MAE only -14.45 and median hold 1.18 minutes, so a blunt global VWAP cap is inappropriate.
strategy_hypotheses.name: Extended-VWAP Tail-State Split
strategy_hypotheses.risk: A crude global threshold would remove fast profitable extension trades and reduce risk-adjusted score despite improving apparent MAE.
strategy_hypotheses.trigger: Entries matching extended-VWAP rejection near misses with delayed early progress, weak support, or excessive wall distance.
strategy_hypotheses.validation_metric: Worst MAE improves toward <= -527, total PnL retention >=97%, and protected extended-VWAP winner PnL retention >=90%.

new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: New profile independently positive, reduces combined charm/pin worst MAE, preserves >=95% of protected fast extended-VWAP winner PnL.

new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Minimum 5 trades over expanded sample, positive PnL, average MAE below active-profile median, and no degradation in core VALID_0DTE profiles.
